lesson.menu_title: Fiscal and Tax Talk -- Communicating With Accountants
lesson.menu_desc: Talking to your accountants about deadlines, audits and compliance -- present perfect with for/since -- 30 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 12 -- Fiscal and Tax Talk -- Communicating With Accountants
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maria Claudia Curimbaba | Aula 12 -- Fiscal and Tax Talk

listenings.text: Hello Maria Claudia, it is Marcelo, your accountant. A quick update before our call this afternoon on where the holding stands for the fiscal year. The good news first: the corporate tax return has already been filed, two days before the deadline, so we are clear there. The annual audit starts on Monday, and I have been gathering the documents since the beginning of the month, so we are well prepared. A few points for you. First, taxes are withheld at source on the dividends, so please do not count that as cash we still owe. Second, the consulting fees are fully deductible this year, but only if we keep every invoice, so I need the originals from your side. Third, I have not reconciled the December bank statement yet, because two transfers are still missing in the ledger. And finally, we have complied with all the new reporting rules for three years now, so the audit should be smooth. Talk soon, and please bring your questions to the call.

Right, let me get my head straight before I call Marcelo. I have worked with this accounting firm since two thousand and fifteen, so for almost a decade now, and honestly I have learned to trust them. Where do we stand? The tax return has been filed, that is the big one off the list. The audit starts on Monday, and the documents have been gathered, so that part is under control. What worries me is the detail. I always make the same mistake on these calls: I say I work with them since twenty fifteen, when I should say I have worked with them since twenty fifteen, because it started in the past and it is still true today. So today I will be careful with for and since. My plan is simple. I will confirm which costs are deductible, I will send Marcelo the missing invoices, and I will ask him why the December statement has not been reconciled yet. We have been compliant for years, and I intend to keep it that way. Let me dial in.

extra_audio.text: First, the accountant tells you what has already been done: the return has been filed and the books have been closed for the period. Then he flags what is still open, such as a bank statement that has not been reconciled yet. Next, he reminds you of the rules: which costs are deductible, and how taxes are withheld at source. After that, he gives you the deadline and asks you to send any missing invoices before the audit. Finally, you agree on a date to review everything together, well before the fiscal year closes.
